mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present value of S&P 500 constituent earnings. A 50 basis point drop in FEDFUNDS over 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive 20-day forward returns through lower cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.28% on 2008-04-01 reflects post-crisis easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS decline periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. A 0.5 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 2 months triggers a 5-day negative signal.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% on 2008-04-01 during early financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer demand strength and future corporate revenue. Sustained month-over-month decline in RSAFS of more than 2% signals demand contraction that reduces forward earnings estimates, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 378148M on 2008-04-01 showing early demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite demand contraction signal, or correlation between RSAFS growth and forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. When INDPRO growth turns negative year-over-year, it signals supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative 20-day forward returns as investors reprrice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.18 on 2008-04-01 showing early supply stress
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day returns during negative INDPRO growth periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal labor market health. When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously, it indicates accelerating job losses that trigger a sharp negative repricing of equity risk premiums, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month decline > 0.2% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137818K and UNRATE at 5.0% on 2008-04-01
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure consumer and construction demand. When both decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, it signals broad-based demand collapse that produces negative 60-day forward returns as forward earnings estimates compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% AND RSAFS declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 947K and RSAFS at 378148M on 2008-04-01 during housing crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) when controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) compresses real returns and forces further rate increases, producing negative 20-day forward returns. This mechanism is regime-conditional and inactive when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.74 and FEDFUNDS at 2.28% on 2008-04-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: The mechanism produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the directional signal reverses (becomes positive) when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. When annualized GDP growth falls below 1.5%, it signals near-recession conditions that produce negative 60-day forward returns as equity risk premiums expand and earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11693.1B on 2008-01-01 showing early slowdown
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 60-day returns during low-growth periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
